mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate directly reduce corporate borrowing costs and compress equity discount rates, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns following rate cuts are negative in >55% of cases over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month increase in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.2 percentage points signals deteriorating labor market conditions and rising recession probability, driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Positive 20-day forward returns occur in >60% of instances where the unemployment rate crosses the 0.2pp threshold.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous month-over-month growth in retail sales and housing starts confirms broad-based consumer demand strength, which supports corporate earnings revisions and produces positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint positive signal yields negative 60-day forward returns in >50% of triggered cases across a 5-year backtest.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating industrial production during a post-shock recovery phase reflects rapid capacity utilization and supply-side momentum, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns following industrial production acceleration are negative in >55% of cases during identified recovery regimes.

mechanism_narrative: Consumer price index growth exceeding 2.5% year-over-year triggers expectations of aggressive central bank tightening, which compresses equity valuations and produces negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns remain positive in >65% of instances when CPI growth breaches the 2.5% threshold.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent month-over-month increases in total nonfarm payrolls and decreases in the unemployment rate confirm labor market tightening, which supports wage growth and consumer spending, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The combined labor signal fails to predict positive 60-day returns in >50% of occurrences over a rolling 4-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Quarter-over-quarter growth in real gross domestic product directly correlates with corporate revenue expansion and earnings upgrades, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns following positive GDP growth are negative in >55% of cases over a 3-year rolling evaluation period.

mechanism_narrative: Retail sales declining by more than 0.5% month-over-month signals immediate consumer pullback, which forces earnings downgrades and produces negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Positive 20-day returns occur in >60% of cases when retail sales drop below the specified threshold.
